title: Tree navigation UI in admin panel sidebar
description: Build the sidebar tree view (sections → pages → tabs) in the admin panel, styled after Material/Atlassian docs navigation, supporting create/rename/reorder/delete actions inline.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar renders sections as top-level collapsible groups, each containing pages, each page containing tabs
Each level has a contextual '+' action to create a child (new page under a section, new tab under a page)
Drag-and-drop or up/down controls allow reordering within the same parent, calling the corresponding `reorder` mutation
Rename is available inline (e.g. double-click or an edit icon) for sections, pages, and tabs
Delete requires a confirmation step before calling the destructive mutation (cascade delete, per TASK-18)
Clicking a tab navigates to its content editor route

steps.details: Create a hook combining `sections.list`, and per-section `pages.listBySection`, and per-page `tabs.listByPage` (or a single denormalized query if preferred) into a nested tree structure for rendering.
Build `apps/admin/src/features/navigation/SidebarTree.tsx` rendering sections/pages/tabs as nested collapsible groups with appropriate indentation and icons.
Add a '+' button per level opening an inline text input for the new item's title (and slug for pages). Add an edit affordance per item for renaming.
Implement drag-and-drop (or simpler up/down buttons if drag-and-drop is out of scope for this pass) within a parent, calling the relevant `reorder` mutation with the new ordered id list on drop.
Add a delete icon per item opening a confirmation dialog warning that child content will also be removed (cascade), calling `delete` only on confirm.

steps.description: Wire tab click to content editor route
steps.details: Clicking a tab navigates to `/admin/pages/:pageId/tabs/:tabId` (or equivalent), where the content editor (Fase 3) will render.
Using Playwright: create a section, a page inside it, a tab inside that page; rename each; reorder two sections; delete a tab and confirm the confirmation dialog blocks accidental deletion.

technicalNotes: This is the highest-complexity UI task in Fase 2 — consider building create/rename/delete first without drag-and-drop, then layering reorder interaction once the tree renders correctly.
Fully expanded trees for large design systems could get long — consider persisting collapse/expand state in local storage, though this is a nice-to-have, not a hard requirement.
